title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Virginia
description: Voters in Virginia will elect one member to the U.S. Senate on November 3, 2026. The race will decide the Class II seat held by incumbent Democrat Mark Warner, who is seeking re-election, and is expected to center on the state’s response to federal budget cuts, health care costs, and national political control.
polling_note: No polls published after 2026-04-01 were found in the available search results.

candidates.name: Mark Warner
candidates.summary: Mark Warner is the incumbent Democratic U.S. senator from Virginia, first elected in 2008 and seeking another term in 2026. In April 2026, he and Sen. Tim Kaine highlighted the impact of federal budget and Medicaid cuts on Virginia hospitals, and separately warned that the Trump administration’s Iran policy was raising costs for Virginians.

summary_sources.title: Warner files for reelection, launching bid for fourth U.S. Senate term

career_history.title: United States Senator
career_history.description: Represents Virginia in the U.S. Senate; serves as vice chairman of the Senate Intelligence Committee and on Finance, Banking, Budget, and Rules.

career_history.title: Technology and business executive
career_history.description: Worked for about 20 years in technology and business; early investor in cellular telephone business and co-founder of a company that became Nextel.

candidates.name: Jason Reynolds
candidates.summary: Jason Reynolds is a Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate in Virginia who says he is running as a progressive alternative to incumbent Mark Warner. His campaign platform emphasizes democracy reform, universal health care, reproductive rights, labor and tax policy, and he says he will not accept corporate or super PAC money.

career_history.title: Project/program manager
career_history.description: Has worked for the last decade as a project/program manager.

career_history.title: Wireless telecommunications studies writer
career_history.description: Returned to work writing wireless telecommunications studies after being a stay-at-home dad.

career_history.title: Stay-at-home dad
career_history.description: Stayed home to care for his children for nine years.

career_history.title: SIGINT analyst
career_history.description: Had a brief stint in the Army as a SIGINT analyst and was honorably discharged.

candidates.name: Kim Farington
candidates.summary: Kim Farington is the Republican candidate for U.S. Senate in Virginia. Her campaign biography says she is a CPA, inventor, entrepreneur, and former senior government financial manager who led work at several federal agencies before founding a cybersecurity and financial management firm.
